title: evaluate() returns Vec but always single element; API misleads callers
description: evaluate() returns Vec<SieveAction> but the implementation always returns exactly one element (eval_script returns vec\![action]). The doc comment says 'current implementation returns at most one action' and acknowledges RFC 5228 permits multiple actions. MAINTENANCE CONSEQUENCE: any caller who writes code that iterates the Vec and handles multiple actions will be surprised when multi-action semantics are added — or will write code that only looks at [0] and breaks when semantics change. The API surface is committed before the semantics are decided. Options: return a single SieveAction and change to Vec later (breaking), or define and implement multi-action semantics now.
